channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Get ready for Google I/O 2026
item.description: Google I/O 2026 is scheduled for May 19-20, with livestreams covering updates on Android, AI, Chrome, and Cloud. Registration is now open.

item.title: Turn creative prompts into interactive XR experiences with Gemini
item.description: The Android XR team is leveraging Gemini's Canvas feature to simplify the creation of interactive XR experiences, allowing rapid prototyping on Samsung Galaxy XR headsets.

item.title: On-Device Function Calling in Google AI Edge Gallery
item.description: Google has launched FunctionGemma, a 270M parameter model for on-device function calling, enabling efficient AI tasks offline on mobile devices.

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now includes a variety of third-party tool integrations, allowing developers to enhance their AI agents with real-world applications.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 will feature a 'save the date' puzzle experience that showcases how AI can enhance event engagement before the main event.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas emphasizes in-person networking and hands-on problem solving, focusing on agentic AI and technical advancements.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: TensorFlow 2.21 introduces LiteRT, a new framework for faster GPU and NPU acceleration, enhancing support for GenAI and on-device inference.

item.title: SharePoint: Extending AI in SharePoint using custom skills
item.description: SharePoint is enhancing its AI capabilities with customizable skills that allow businesses to tailor solutions to their specific needs.

item.title: Microsoft Teams: Improved organization for muted and meeting chats
item.description: Microsoft Teams is introducing new chat organization features that group muted and meeting chats, streamlining user experience.

item.title: Microsoft Viva: Following feed in Viva Engage
item.description: Microsoft Viva Engage is launching a new feed type to help users keep up with posts from followed communities and leaders.

item.title: Microsoft Purview: Data Catalog – Migration tool in Unified Catalog to migrate classic glossary terms to Unified Catalog
item.description: Microsoft introduced a migration tool for its Unified Catalog, allowing users to transfer classic glossary terms and associated relationships. This tool aims to streamline data management processes.

item.title: Microsoft Teams: Facilitator detects and answers questions
item.description: Microsoft Teams' new Facilitator feature automatically detects unanswered questions during meetings and offers to find answers via web search. This aims to improve information flow in discussions.

item.title: Anthropic took down thousands of GitHub repos trying to yank its leaked source code — a move the company says was an accident
item.description: Anthropic accidentally took down thousands of GitHub repositories in an attempt to protect its leaked source code but later retracted most of the takedown notices. This incident highlights the challenges of managing intellectual property.

item.title: Meta’s natural gas binge could power South Dakota
item.description: Meta's new Hyperion AI data center will be powered by ten natural gas plants in South Dakota, reflecting the company's commitment to expanding its AI infrastructure. This move raises questions about energy sustainability.

item.title: Holo3: Breaking the Computer Use Frontier
item.description: The article titled 'Holo3: Breaking the Computer Use Frontier' lacks content for summarization. Further details are needed to provide insights.

item.title: Cognichip wants AI to design the chips that power AI, and just raised $60M to try
item.description: Cognichip has raised $60 million to develop AI-designed chips, claiming it can significantly reduce development costs and timelines. This innovation could transform the chip manufacturing industry.

item.title: Less than a month: StrictlyVC San Francisco brings leaders from TDK Ventures, Replit, and more together
item.description: StrictlyVC San Francisco is hosting an event on April 30, featuring leaders from various tech companies. The event aims to foster networking and collaboration in the tech community.

item.title: Wednesday: Hili dialogue
item.description: The article titled 'Wednesday: Hili dialogue' contains no relevant content for summarization. Further information is required.

item.title: Nvidia commits billions to Lumentum, Synopsys, Nokia, XAI, OpenAI, Intel in March alone
item.description: Nvidia has committed billions to several companies, including Lumentum and OpenAI, in March alone, indicating its aggressive investment strategy in the tech sector. This could enhance Nvidia's influence in AI and related technologies.

item.title: tramontane 0.2.0
item.description: The article discusses tramontane 0.2.0, an orchestration framework designed for EU sovereignty and GDPR compliance. This framework aims to enhance data privacy and security.

item.title: Are platforms complying with India’s new deepfake and AI content rules?
item.description: India's new IT rules regarding deepfake and AI content are now in effect, but compliance from platforms remains uncertain, raising key questions about enforcement.

item.title: discourse-sim added to PyPI
item.description: The discourse-sim library has been added to PyPI, enabling agent-based social simulations to model public discourse dynamics after significant events.

item.title: hugging-mapper 1.0.2
item.description: Hugging-mapper 1.0.2 is a new lightweight Python tool designed for easy text similarity scoring using Hugging Face models.

item.title: Oracle Layoffs explained: 'Fire humans, build AI', video explains how 30,000 job cut could become a template for tech layoffs
item.description: Oracle is laying off around 12,000 employees in India as part of a larger global reduction of 30,000 roles, reallocating funds to AI infrastructure.

item.title: appgenerator-cli 1.1.2
item.description: The appgenerator-cli 1.1.2 tool has been released, providing developers with a command-line interface for FastAPI and LangChain projects.

item.title: pydefend 1.0.0
item.description: Pydefend 1.0.0 introduces AI security guardrails for LLM applications, allowing users to scan inputs and check outputs across various platforms.

item.title: Judge Halts Construction of Trump Ballroom, Maurizio Cattelan Wants You to Confess… to Him: Morning Links for April 1, 2026
item.description: This article discusses various unrelated topics, including a halted construction project and an art installation by Maurizio Cattelan.

item.title: Oracle believed to have cut 10,000 positions across multiple divisions as mass layoffs begin — company is reportedly reducing headcount as it makes massive investments in AI
item.description: Reports indicate that Oracle has cut over 10,000 positions as part of its strategy to invest heavily in AI, potentially impacting its financial health.

item.title: IT sector set for April rebound on better earnings: VK Vijayakumar
item.description: Analyst VK Vijayakumar predicts a rebound in the IT sector in April, driven by improved earnings amid current market volatility.

item.title: Show HN: ShellAgent – Open-source agentic terminal, bring your own API
item.description: ShellAgent is an open-source agentic terminal that allows users to bring their own API, enhancing customization and functionality.

item.title: Apple at 50: where does it go from here?
item.description: Apple celebrates its 50th anniversary while facing challenges like AI integration, trade tensions, and the need for innovation to remain relevant. The company's future direction will be crucial for its continued influence in tech.

item.title: Dew Drop – April 1, 2026 (#4637)
item.description: This article provides a roundup of updates and links related to technology, including Visual Studio enhancements and insights into Claude Code's functionalities. It serves as a resource for developers looking to stay informed.

item.title: Show HN: LLM Gateway – Simple API format converter for LLM providers
item.description: The LLM Gateway is introduced as a simple API format converter for LLM providers, aimed at streamlining interactions with various language models. This tool could enhance accessibility for developers working with LLMs.

item.title: Show HN: Muscula – Lightweight error monitoring with CLI and MCP
item.description: Muscula is a lightweight error monitoring tool that tracks errors across multiple programming languages. It provides essential features like error grouping and uptime checks, catering to developers seeking efficient monitoring solutions.

item.title: Leaked DeepSeek V4 Benchmarks Reveal a Massive 1-Million Token Context Window
item.description: Leaked benchmarks for DeepSeek V4 reveal a model with a massive 1-million token context window and a novel architecture that could enhance multimodal capabilities. This development has generated significant interest in the AI community.

item.title: The Model You Love Is Probably Just the One You Use
item.description: This article discusses the subjective nature of LLM recommendations among developers, emphasizing that personal usage often drives preferences rather than objective comparisons. It highlights the diversity of opinions in the LLM space.

item.title: Goldman Sachs Upgrades Nokia (NOK) to Neutral, Raises PT
item.description: Goldman Sachs upgraded Nokia's stock rating from Sell to Neutral, indicating a more favorable outlook for the company. This change reflects confidence in Nokia's potential for growth in the tech market.

item.title: ramjetio 0.5.1
item.description: Ramjetio 0.5.1 is released as a distributed cache system designed for PyTorch training, aiming to optimize performance in machine learning tasks. This tool could enhance efficiency for developers using PyTorch.

item.title: torchoptics 1.0.0
item.description: Torchoptics 1.0.0 is introduced as a Python library for differentiable Fourier optics simulations using PyTorch. This library offers new capabilities for researchers and developers in optics and machine learning.

item.title: Kala Bio Engages Dr. Saeid Babaei as Senior Scientific Advisor to Evaluate Clinical Assets and Precision Development Opportunities
item.description: Kala Bio has engaged Dr. Saeid Babaei as a Senior Scientific Advisor to evaluate its clinical assets and explore AI-enabled development opportunities. This move aims to enhance the company's strategic direction in precision medicine.

item.title: litert-torch-nightly 0.9.0.dev20260401
item.description: LiteRT has released version 0.9.0.dev20260401, which now supports the conversion of PyTorch models. This update enhances compatibility for developers using PyTorch.

item.title: Meta’s Two New Smart Glasses are Made for Prescription Users
item.description: Meta has launched two new Ray-Ban smart glasses tailored for users with prescription lenses. This move expands their product line to cater to a broader audience.

item.title: Meta Inches Into Health Wearables with New Food Logging Feature for Ray-Ban Smart Glasses
item.description: Meta is updating its Ray-Ban and Oakley smart glasses with a food logging feature that allows users to track nutrition through AI food recognition. This aims to simplify health management for users.

item.title: DXPR: Talk to Your Drupal Page Builder DXPR Builder 2.8 AI Release!
item.description: DXPR has released the 2.8 version of its Builder tool, introducing significant AI capabilities for Drupal page building. This update aims to enhance user interaction and efficiency.

item.title: Final Four predictions: AI picks winners of Illinois-UConn, Arizona-Michigan games
item.description: AI predictions for the 2026 Men's NCAA Tournament Final Four games have been released, showcasing the technology's capability in sports forecasting. This reflects the growing influence of AI in sports analytics.

item.title: Global Industry Analysts, Inc., Rebrands to Market Glass, Inc., an Agentic AI Platform
item.description: Global Industry Analysts, Inc. has rebranded to Market Glass, Inc., launching an agentic AI platform for market analysis. This aims to provide more insightful and validated reports.

item.title: Salesforce reveals major AI overhaul for 'ultimate teammate' Slackbot, with over 30 new features
item.description: Salesforce has unveiled a major AI overhaul for its Slackbot, introducing over 30 new features aimed at enhancing user collaboration and productivity. This positions Slack as a more powerful tool for teams.

item.title: Okta's CEO says all AI agents need a kill switch
item.description: Okta's CEO emphasized the necessity of a 'kill switch' for all AI agents to ensure security and control. This highlights ongoing concerns about AI safety in the workplace.

item.title: Google opens applications for AI-focused startups accelerator in India - Here is how to apply
item.description: Google has opened applications for its AI-focused startup accelerator in India, providing resources and support for AI-first companies. This initiative aims to foster innovation in the region.

item.title: (PR) ASUS Announces UGen300 USB AI Accelerator
item.description: ASUS has announced the UGen300 USB AI Accelerator, its first AI USB device designed to enhance inference performance on any device. This compact accelerator aims to broaden AI accessibility.
